title: 9 MTS Branch Management for Wires
body_text:
9	MTS Branch Management for Wires
9.1	Overview of MTS Branch Management for Wires
MTS Administrators use the branch settings to manage the amount at which outgoing wires require one or two approvals.
The Wires processing environment your financial institution selects determines if your branches are linked and if you enter MTS branch settings for all branches individually, or just for the head office. For example, in a centralized Wires environment, you would enter the MTS branch settings for the head office and not for the individual branches. See Chapter 5, Selecting a Wires Processing Model, for a description of a centralized, partially centralized, and decentralized Wires environment.
Note: 	For linked transits, wires limits are based on the Wires UserГўВҖВҷs home transit.
Your financial institution must set Wires authorization limits in MTS branch settings before using Wires for the first time and before creating outgoing wires for a new branch. Practice due diligence when establishing Wires authorization limits. Ensure that two authorized users must approve all large value wires.
9.2	Description of Queues in the MTS Branch Tab
The following queues display on the Branch tab. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Queues in the Branch Tab
Branches Queue
ГўВҖВ“ Select
Branches
to view your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs branches.
Note:	Multiple branches display if your home transit number is linked to other branches, and you have permission to access these branches.
Change List Queue
ГўВҖВ“ Select
Change List
to view branch changes that havenГўВҖВҷt been authorized.
Related Information
Training Videos:
Using MTS for Wires User and Branch Settings
9.3	Description of Columns in the MTS Branch Queue
The following table describes the Branch queue columns. See Figure 2.
Column Name
Description
Transit
The branch transit number.
Credit Union
Your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs name.
Branch
The branch name.
Street
The branch street address.
Town
The branch city.
Province
The branch province.
Figure 2: Columns in the Branch Queue
Related Information
Training Videos:
Using MTS for Wires User and Branch Settings
9.4	Viewing Branch Settings in MTS
An MTS Administrator views branch settings as follows:
Log in to MTS as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to MTS, then select the
Branch
tab. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Select Branch Tab
Select the Transit Number of the branch you want to view. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Select Branch
Tip
:
A branch wonГўВҖВҷt appear in the Branch queue if there are pending changes to the branch settings. Authorize or cancel pending changes before proceeding.
Note:	Multiple branches display in the drop-down menu if other transit numbers are linked to your home transit number, and you have permission to access the linked branches.
Related Information
Training Videos:
Using MTS for Wires User and Branch Settings
9.5	Entering Branch Settings in MTS
An MTS Administrator enters branch settings as follows:
Log in to MTS as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to MTS, then select the
Branch
tab. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Select Branch Tab
Select the transit number of the branch you want to update. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Select Branch
Tip
:
A branch wonГўВҖВҷt display in the Branch queue if there are pending changes to the branch settings. Authorize or cancel pending changes before proceeding.
Note:	Multiple branches display in the drop-down menu if other transit numbers are linked to your home transit number, and you have permission to access the linked branches.
Complete the branch settings as follows. See Figure 7.
Important
: 	You must practice due diligence when establishing authorization levels.
ГўВ‘
Level 1 Authorization
(mandatory)
Branch Transits used for In-branch Wires
ГўВҲВ’
Enter ГўВҖВң1.00ГўВҖВқ. The Level 1 Authorization amount isnГўВҖВҷt considered for in-branch wires, however, as the field is mandatory, you must enter a value.
Note:	All wires created in branch require a minimum of one approval.
Branch Transits used for
MemberDirect
ГӮВ®
Business Services (MDB) / Forge Commercial Wires
ГўВҲВ’
Enter the amount at which your business customerГўВҖВҷs outgoing wires require financial institution approval in the Wires application. Note the following:
ГўВҖВў
Wire amounts less than or equal to the MTS Level 1 Authorization limit donГўВҖВҷt require approval in the Wires application. Wires that donГўВҖВҷt require approval display in the Submitted Wires list in the Wires application. This differs from wires created in-branch. All wires created in-branch require at least one approval, regardless of the amount.
ГўВҖВў
Wire amounts greater than the MTS Level 1 Authorization limit, up to and including the Level 2 Authorization limit, require one approval in the Wires application.
Note:	Central 1 recommends entering ГўВҖВң1.00ГўВҖВқ so that all wires require at least one financial institution approval. See Chapter 29, Using MemberDirect
ГӮВ®
Business (MDB) / Forge Commercial to Create Wires, for more information.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Level 2 Authorization
(mandatory)
Branch Transits used for In-branch Wires
ГўВҲВ’
Enter the amount at which outgoing wires created in branch require two approvals in the Wires application. Note the following:
ГўВҖВў
Wire amounts less than or equal to the Level 2 Authorization limit always require one approval, regardless of the amount you entered for your Level 1 Authorization limit.
ГўВҖВў
Wire amounts greater than the Level 2 Authorization limit require two approvals.
Important
:	If the same transit is used for in-branch wires and MDB / Forge Commercial Wires, the amount applies to both in-branch and business customer wires.
Branch Transits used for
MemberDirect
ГӮВ®
Business Services (MDB) / Forge Commercial Wires
ГўВҲВ’
Enter the amount at which your business customerГўВҖВҷs outgoing wires require two financial institution approvals in the Wires application. Wire amounts greater than the MTS Level 2 Authorization Limit require two approvals.
Important
:	Ensure that the MTS authorization limits are set before the first outgoing wire is created.
Important
:	Authorization limits are in Canadian dollars. Wires converts the amount of an outgoing wire in foreign funds (including US dollars) to Canadian dollars at the live rate to compare the wire amount to the authorization limits. If live rates arenГўВҖВҷt available, the last available rate is used. This conversion only determines the number of approvals required for foreign funds outgoing wires and doesnГўВҖВҷt affect the currency of the outgoing wire.
ГўВ‘Вў
Select the
Linked
check box beside a branch to link the branches listed at the bottom of the page to the branch listed in the FIF Information section, if applicable. Wires Users and MTS Administrators who have the transit number displayed in the FIF Information section as their home transit can access the linked branches if theyГўВҖВҷre assigned the ГўВҖВңUse Linked TransitsГўВҖВқ permission on their MTS profile.
Note: 	For linked transits, authorization limits are based on the userГўВҖВҷs home transit.
Repeat the above process for each branch if Wires Users and MTS Administrators from each branch are authorized to access all other branches.
Note:	The Financial Institution fields arenГўВҖВҷt editable. They are obtained from the Payment CanadaГўВҖВҷs Financial Institution File (FIF).
The Member Charge and Non-Member Charge fields donГўВҖВҷt apply. Leave them blank. Outgoing wire fees are managed in the Wires Application. See Chapter 11, Managing Outgoing Wire Fees, for procedures.
Select
Update
.
Figure 7: Enter Branch Settings
Have another MTS Administrator authorize the change. See Section 9.7, Authorizing Changes to Branch Settings in MTS.
Related Information
Training Videos:
Using MTS for Wires User and Branch Settings
9.6	Description of Columns in the MTS Change List Queue (Branches)
The Change List on the Branch tab provides a list of unauthorized branch changes. The following table describes Change List queue columns. See Figure 8.
Column Name
Description
Transit
The branch transit number.
Credit Union
Your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs name.
Branch
The branch name.
Street
The branch street address.
Town
The branch city.
Province
The branch province.
Action
This column always displays ГўВҖВңUpdatedГўВҖВқ. MTS Administrators canГўВҖВҷt add or delete branches.
Figure 8: Change List
Related Information
Training Videos:
Using MTS for Wires User and Branch Settings
9.7	Authorizing Changes to Branch Settings in MTS
A second MTS Administrator must authorize all branch changes. To authorize a change:
Log in to MTS as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to MTS, then select the
Branch
tab. See Figure 9.
Tip
:	The number next to ГўВҖВңBranch ChangesГўВҖВқ reflects the number of unauthorized branch changes.
Figure 9: Access Branch Tab
Select
Change List
. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: Access Change List
Select the transit number of the branch you want to authorize. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Select Branch
Review the change and select
Authorize
. See Figure 12. Alternatively, select
Cancel
to delete the change.
Figure 12: Authorize Change
Related Information
Training Videos:
Using MTS for Wires User and Branch Settings
END
